title: Developing the Pipeline
text: parameterized SQL queries used SqlRender, create files analysis/src/sql/ using makeSrcSqlFile(): creates standardized SQL file analysis/src/sql/drug_exposure_summary.sql placeholders SqlRender parameters. Important: SqlRender uses @paramName notation substitution. ’s realistic example builds intermediate work table multi-step logic: Best Practice: Wrap SQL queries utility functions stored analysis/src/ files, call functions tasks. keeps SQL execution logic organized reusable: call function task file: Workflow Summary: 1. SQL file creates intermediate work table computed values 2. R function orchestrates render → translate → execute → collect 3. Task file sources utility function gets back dataframe 4. database configuration comes ExecutionSettings
code: makeSrcSqlFile(   fileName = "drug_exposure_summary",   author = "Jane Doe",   description = "Create a summary table of drug exposure by observation period" ) /*  Document your parameters at the top:   @cdmDatabaseSchema - Schema containing OMOP CDM tables   @workDatabaseSchema - Schema for output tables   @workTableName - Name of the intermediate work table to create   @studyStartDate - Start date for filtering   @studyEndDate - End date for filtering */  -- Create intermediate table of drug exposures by observation period CREATE TABLE @workDatabaseSchema.@workTableName AS  WITH obs_periods AS (   -- Get all observation periods for study participants   SELECT      person_id,     observation_period_start_date,     observation_period_end_date   FROM @cdmDatabaseSchema.observation_period   WHERE observation_period_start_date >= '@studyStartDate'     AND observation_period_start_date <= '@studyEndDate' ),  drug_exp AS (   -- Get drug exposures during these observation periods   SELECT     d.person_id,     op.observation_period_start_date,     op.observation_period_end_date,     d.drug_concept_id,     d.drug_exposure_start_date,     COALESCE(d.drug_exposure_end_date, d.drug_exposure_start_date) AS drug_exposure_end_date,     COALESCE(d.days_supply, 1) AS days_supply   FROM @cdmDatabaseSchema.drug_exposure d   JOIN obs_periods op     ON d.person_id = op.person_id     AND d.drug_exposure_start_date >= op.observation_period_start_date     AND d.drug_exposure_start_date <= op.observation_period_end_date ),  person_summary AS (   -- Aggregate to person-level summaries   SELECT     person_id,     observation_period_start_date,     observation_period_end_date,     COUNT(DISTINCT drug_concept_id) AS num_unique_drugs,     COUNT(DISTINCT drug_exposure_start_date) AS num_drug_exposures,     SUM(days_supply) AS total_days_supply,     AVG(days_supply) AS avg_days_supply,     MIN(drug_exposure_start_date) AS first_drug_date,     MAX(drug_exposure_end_date) AS last_drug_date   FROM drug_exp   GROUP BY person_id, observation_period_start_date, observation_period_end_date )  SELECT * FROM person_summary; # Example utility function in analysis/src/execute_sql_queries.R  get_drug_exposure_summary <- function(executionSettings,                                       workTableName = "drug_exposure_summary",                                       studyStartDate = "2020-01-01",                                       studyEndDate = "2023-12-31") {   # Read the SQL file   sql <- readr::read_file(here::here("analysis/src/sql/drug_exposure_summary.sql"))      # Step 1: Render parameters using SqlRender   rendered_drug_sql <- SqlRender::render(     sql,     cdmDatabaseSchema = executionSettings$cdmDatabaseSchema,     workDatabaseSchema = executionSettings$workDatabaseSchema,     workTableName = workTableName,     studyStartDate = studyStartDate,     studyEndDate = studyEndDate   )      # Step 2: Translate to DBMS dialect   # The ExecutionSettings object knows your DBMS type and tempEmulationSchema   translated_drug_sql <- SqlRender::translate(     rendered_drug_sql,     targetDialect = executionSettings$getDbms(),     tempEmulationSchema = executionSettings$tempEmulationSchema   )      # Get the connection from ExecutionSettings   connection <- executionSettings$getConnection()      # If connection is null, establish one   if (is.null(connection)) {     executionSettings$connect()     connection <- executionSettings$getConnection()   }      # Step 3: Execute the query to create the work table   DatabaseConnector::executeSql(connection, translated_drug_sql)      # Step 4: Query the created table to retrieve results   # Use SqlRender and translate for the SELECT query as well   select_query <- "SELECT * FROM @workDatabaseSchema.@workTableName"      rendered_select <- SqlRender::render(     select_query,     workDatabaseSchema = executionSettings$workDatabaseSchema,     workTableName = workTableName   )      translated_select <- SqlRender::translate(     rendered_select,     targetDialect = executionSettings$getDbms(),     tempEmulationSchema = executionSettings$tempEmulationSchema   )      results <- DatabaseConnector::querySql(connection, translated_select)      # Disconnect from the database   executionSettings$disconnect()      cli::cli_alert_success("Drug exposure summary retrieved successfully")      return(results) } # In your task file (e.g., 02_drug_exposure_analysis.R) source(here::here("analysis/src/execute_sql_queries.R"))  # Execute the SQL query and collect results drug_summary <- get_drug_exposure_summary(   executionSettings = executionSettings,   workTableName = "drug_exp_summary",   studyStartDate = "2020-01-01",   studyEndDate = "2023-12-31" )  # Now work with results in R head(drug_summary)

title: Evidence Generation Plan
text: Evidence Generation Plan (EGP) required document Picard pipeline. structured specification document bridges research questions analysis code. EGP defines analytical experiment layman’s terms drives pipeline—code implements specifications document . serves three critical purposes: Precision: Translates research questions exact analytical specifications (numerators, denominators, observation windows, inclusion/exclusion logic) Transparency: Makes choices explicit justified, enabling others understand validate approach Implementation: Provides sufficient detail programmers can implement vision without ambiguity Key insight: EGPs one-size-fits-templates. adapt fundamentally based study design: Surveillance studies (prevalence, incidence) emphasize observation periods, lookback windows, numerator/denominator specifications (example: Type 2 Diabetes prevalence) Characterization studies describe patient populations, baseline features, treatment patterns, temporal trends (example: Psoriasis patients) Outcome studies focus exposure definitions, time--risk, outcome specifications Treatment pathway studies detail era logic, gap collapsing rules, sequence analysis Note EGP file automatically generated launch study. template contains comments guidance help fill details. vignette teaches principles EGP construction shows examples different study types. ’ll adapt structure match research questions.

title: Evidence Generation Plan
text: Define study populations important cohorts precisely. cohort definitions three main components: Entry criteria: patients enter cohort? Inclusion criteria: additional criteria must met stay cohort? Exit criteria: patients exit cohort (.e censored death end continuous observation)? examples cohort definitions can structured based study design: Psoriasis Example: Type 2 Diabetes Example: guiding principles defining cohort definitions EGP: index date clearly defined. Typically index date sequence events second occurrence (avoid immortal time bias). index date occurrence clinical event (diagnosis, procedure, medication dispensing) rather arbitrary date (calendar date, visit). Always provide temporal logic sequence events. example prior treatment state either time window relative index date explicit whether limit first-ever occurrence allow multiple occurrences per patient Clearly define exit criteria (death, end observation) ensure proper censoring follow-analyses Programming tip: Cohort definitions Atlas come markdown format pasted EGP can clean format.
code: ### Newly-Diagnosed Psoriasis Cohort  #### Cohort Entry - First visit date with Psoriasis diagnosis code - Requirement: >= 2 Psoriasis diagnosis visits on different dates, 30-365 days apart - Requirement: 365 days continuous prior observation - Limit occurrence to the initial event per patient (first-ever diagnosis)  #### Inclusion Criteria - NO prior psoriasis-related treatments in 365 days prior to index  #### Cohort Exit - End of continuous observation, OR - Death ### Type 2 Diabetes Cohort  #### Cohort Entry - 2 codes of Type 2 Diabetes on different dates, at least 30 days apart - the second code is the index date - Limit occurrence to the initial event per patient (first-ever diagnosis)  #### Inclusion Criteria - NO occurrence of Type 1 Diabetes diagnosis code at any time prior to index date - NO occurrence of Gestational Diabetes diagnosis code at any time prior to index date  #### Cohort Exit - End of continuous observation, OR - Death

title: Evidence Generation Plan

Want convey experiment logic way easy codify experiment set parameters. examples specify analyses different study types. Provide much detail possible analysis. Prevalence Example: Characterization Example:
code: ### Prevalence Calculation: Any-Time Point Estimate  **Period of Interest:** Each calendar year 2015-2024  **Observation Requirements:** - Include: Anyone with >= 1 day enrollment in the year - Minimum continuous: None (any fragmented enrollment allowed)  **Lookback:** - Search 365 days prior to period for any Type 2 Diabetes diagnosis  **Numerator:** - Patients with Type 2 Diabetes diagnosis code prior to OR during period  **Denominator:** - All patients with any enrollment during period of interest  **Calculation:** Prevalence = (Numerator / Denominator) per 100,000 ### Baseline Characteristics  **Baseline Window:** 365 days prior to index date through 1 day prior  **Demographics Reported:** - Age at index date - Sex, Race, Ethnicity - Payer type - Calendar year of index  **Comorbidities:** - Report any diagnosis code during baseline window for:   - Autoimmune/Inflammatory conditions (Rheumatoid arthritis, Crohn's disease, etc.)   - Cardiovascular disease   - Metabolic disease (Diabetes, Obesity)   - [Organized by clinical system]  **Treatments:** - Any dispensing during baseline window for:   - Topical agents (corticosteroids, calcineurin inhibitors, retinoids)   - Systemic agents (methotrexate, acitretin, cyclosporine, apremilast)   - Biologic agents (TNF inhibitors, IL-17i, IL-23i, JAK inhibitors)   - Other (phototherapy, oral steroids)  ### Treatment Pathways During Follow-up  **Gap Collapsing Rules:** - Topical agents: Collapse gaps of <30 days - Systemic agents: Collapse gaps of <30-60 days depending on agent - Biologic agents: Collapse gaps of <60-120 days depending on specific drug  **Combination Logic:** - Different drug classes overlapping >= 60 days = counted as combination therapy  **Episode Minimum:** - Episodes of <14 days are excluded (too short to assess effectiveness)  **Output:** - Sunburst plot showing first 4 treatment steps in newly-diagnosed population - Table: Median days on treatment by therapy class and line number

previous_headings: Why Git and renv Matter for Picard Studies
title: Launching a Picard Study
text: Git tracks every change code documentation throughout project lifecycle. pipeline-driven studies, provides critical benefits: Code reproducibility: Git records exactly version code produced results. essential regulatory compliance peer review. Audit trail: Every commit includes made change, , . accountability crucial study documentation QC. Collaboration: Multiple team members can work different analysis tasks simultaneously without conflicts. Git helps merge changes cleanly. Pipeline provenance: pipeline generates results, can trace results back exact code commit produced . Disaster recovery: Git acts backup. something goes wrong, can revert previous working state. Feature branches: can test new analysis approaches isolated branches merging production. Picard’s Branching Model: Picard enforces strict branching workflow makes Git requirement, optional: Main branch: Protected branch used release-ready code. Production pipelines executed release branches created main. Develop branch: Integration branch team members merge tested features. testing QC happens code ready production. Feature/task branches: Individual developers work analysis tasks isolated branches, submit pull requests review merging develop. branching strategy ensures: - Production work never runs unstable code main - changes reviewed reaching production - Testing happens controlled environment deployment - Team members can work independently without interfering production results Without Git disciplined branching approach, safe way run production pipelines study multiple contributors. studies data security reproducibility paramount, Git optional—’s foundational. Note: Git automatically initialized create repository. specify gitRemote setup, need manually connect local repository remote push changes. See Setting Git Version Control section instructions.

title: Launching a Picard Study
text: R packages constantly updated. Different versions can produce different results, even identical data code. renv solves creating snapshot R environment: Reproducibility across time: renv.lock captures exact package versions used analysis. Six months six years later, can restore identical environment reproduce every result. Team consistency: collaborative studies, different team members might different package versions installed. renv ensures everyone uses versions, eliminating “works machine” problems. Dependency management: renv tracks just direct dependencies nested dependencies. package depends package B version 1.2, renv captures relationship. Production safety: promoting analysis code production, renv ensures dependencies compatible tested together. Regulatory compliance: studies subject validation requirements, renv provides documented evidence package versions captured reproducible. collaborative, regulated research environments, renv essential ensuring results truly reproducible team member point future. Note: Unlike Git, renv automatically initialized repository. must set renv . highly encouraged : - Ensures analysis produces consistent results run team members - Protects package updates silently break pipeline - Creates audit trail package versions used study See Setting renv Reproducibility section get started.

title: Launching a Picard Study
text: Study metadata describes research project. Create StudyMeta object project information using makeStudyMeta(): Parameters: - studyTitle: Human-readable project name - therapeuticArea: Therapeutic disease area (e.g., “CRM”, “Oncology”, “Cardiology”) - studyType: Type study (e.g., “Characterization”, “Population-Level Estimation”, “Patient-Level Prediction”) - contributors: List contributor profiles created setContributor() - name: Full name - email: Contact email - role: Role type (e.g., “developer”, “qc”, “principal investigator”) - studyTags: Character vector study tags organization
code: library(picard)  sm <- makeStudyMeta(   studyTitle = "Diabetes Characterization Study",   therapeuticArea = "Endocrinology",   studyType = "Characterization",   contributors = list(     setContributor(       name = "Jane Doe",       email = "jane.doe@institution.org",       role = "developer"     ),     setContributor(       name = "John Smith",       email = "john.smith@institution.org",       role = "qc"     )   ),   studyTags = c("OMOP", "OHDSI", "Characterization") )

title: Launching a Picard Study
text: analyzing database (toolType = “dbms”), create database configuration block using setDbConfigBlock(): Parameters: - configBlockName: Identifier database configuration - cdmDatabaseSchema: Schema containing OMOP CDM tables - databaseName: Name database (internal tracking) - cohortTable: Name table cohorts created - databaseLabel: Human-readable label reports documentation multiple databases, create multiple blocks:
code: db <- setDbConfigBlock(   configBlockName = "my_cdm",   cdmDatabaseSchema = "omop_cdm_schema",   databaseName = "my_database_v1",   cohortTable = "study_cohorts",   databaseLabel = "Primary CDM" ) db1 <- setDbConfigBlock(   configBlockName = "my_cdm",   cdmDatabaseSchema = "omop_cdm_schema",   databaseName = "my_database_v1",   cohortTable = "study_cohorts",   databaseLabel = "Primary CDM" )  db2 <- setDbConfigBlock(   configBlockName = "secondary_cdm",   cdmDatabaseSchema = "secondary_omop_schema",   databaseName = "secondary_database_v1",   cohortTable = "study_cohorts_sec",   databaseLabel = "Secondary CDM" )

title: Launching a Picard Study
text: Execution options define pipeline execute (DBMS type, schemas, database connections). Use makeExecOptions(): Parameters: - dbms: Database management system type (e.g., “snowflake”, “postgresql”, “sql server”, “bigquery”) - workDatabaseSchema: Schema creating temporary/working tables - tempEmulationSchema: Schema emulating temporary tables - dbConnectionBlocks: List database configuration blocks created Step 2
code: eo <- makeExecOptions(   dbms = "snowflake",   workDatabaseSchema = "work_schema",   tempEmulationSchema = "work_schema",   dbConnectionBlocks = list(db) )

text: Use resetCohortManifest() need clear cohort data. Choose scope based want preserve:

title: The Manifest: Architecture, Workflows, and Helpers
text: Rebuilding derived pipeline new parameters → "derived". base cohort registrations ATLAS imports preserved; just re-run $build*() calls. Corrupt restructured database → "manifest". Source files kept; call initCohortManifest() re-register via $add*() $importAtlasCohorts(). Complete restart → "full". Requires executionSettings drop OMOP tables. Use caution. scopes prompt confirmation. Pass confirm = FALSE skip scripts.
code: # Rebuild derived pipeline only (keeps base cohorts) resetCohortManifest(manifest = manifest, scope = "derived")  # Wipe manifest DB, keep json/ and sql/ source files resetCohortManifest(cohortsFolderPath = here::here("inputs/cohorts"),                     scope = "manifest")  # Full nuclear reset (also drops OMOP cohort tables) resetCohortManifest(manifest          = manifest,                     scope             = "full",                     executionSettings = execSettings)

title: The Ulysses Standard Repository Structure
text: Picard built Ulysses framework, follows philosophy real-world evidence (RWE) studies organized versioned software projects. Just databases require schema organize data, RWE studies benefit standard directory structure organize code, inputs, analyses, outputs. standardization enables: Reproducibility: Anyone can understand project structure glance Collaboration: Multiple contributors follow conventions Automation: Consistent organization enables reliable workflows tooling Version control: Clear separation concerns makes git history meaningful Picard uses Ulysses repository structure, adding specialized directories configuration cohort-based studies, Evidence Generation Plans, results dissemination. vignette describes standard Ulysses repository structure created initialize project using launchUlyssesRepo().

title: The Ulysses Standard Repository Structure
text: Ulysses repository organizes folders match flow real-world evidence study: Workflow sequence: 1. Initialize metadata (config.yml defines databases credentials) 2. Load create inputs (cohorts concept sets) 3. Execute analysis tasks (code analysis/tasks runs using inputs) 4. Raw results written exec/results organized database version 5. Post-processing (migrations) formatting (excel, CSV) 6. Dissemination via Study Hub (quarto website) formatted exports
code: ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ 1. META & CONFIG                                                │ │    (config.yml, README.md, NEWS.md, main.R, test_main.R)       │ │         ↓                                                        │ │ 2. INPUTS                                                       │ │    inputs/cohorts/ + inputs/conceptSets/                      │ │    (Define phenotypes, cohorts, covariates)                    │ │         ↓                                                        │ │ 3. ANALYSIS                                                     │ │    analysis/tasks/ + analysis/src/ + analysis/migrations/     │ │    (Execute analyses, generate statistics)                      │ │         ↓                                                        │ │ 4. EXECUTION OUTPUT                                             │ │    exec/results/[database]/[version]/[task]/                  │ │    (Raw results by task, database, version)                     │ │         ↓                                                        │ │ 5. DISSEMINATION                                                │ │    dissemination/export/ + dissemination/quarto/               │ │    (Format results, create Study Hub website)                   │ └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

title: The Ulysses Standard Repository Structure
text: newly initialized Picard study following high-level structure:
code: study-repository/ ├── analysis/          # Study analysis code and workflows ├── inputs/            # Cohort definitions and concept sets ├── dissemination/     # Results and evidence outputs ├── exec/              # Execution artifacts and logs ├── docs/              # Generated documentation (pkgdown) ├── extras/            # Reference scripts and development files ├── config.yml         # Project configuration ├── main.R             # Production pipeline execution script ├── README.md          # Project overview ├── NEWS.md            # Release notes and changelog ├── .gitignore         # Git configuration └── study.Rproj        # RStudio project file

title: The Ulysses Standard Repository Structure
text: Central configuration file specifying parameters needed establish database connections. Uses YAML format two section types: default: Universal study settings (project name, version) block headers: Database-specific configurations (dbms, credentials, schemas) source block header task file, pipeline runs using block’s configuration, enabling multi-database studies. Important: Connection details vary database system. codebase distinguishes : Snowflake: Uses connectionString format (JDBC connection string) PostgreSQL, SQL Server, MySQL, Oracle, Redshift: Use server port fields Protecting Credentials !expr: !expr tag (config package) allows evaluate R code within config file. critical security: enables pulling credentials environment variables rather storing plain text config.yml. can use R function wrapped !expr retrieve credentials, including: Environment variables: !expr Sys.getenv('VAR_NAME') Keyring package: !expr keyring::key_get(service = 'picard', username = 'atlasUser') Custom functions: !expr my_secure_fetch_credential('db_password') Best practice: Always use !expr secure credential storage system. Never store passwords connection strings plain text config.yml commit git. Common credentials: dbms: Database type (snowflake, sql server, postgresql, mysql, oracle, redshift) user: Database username (environment variable via !expr Sys.getenv()) password: Database password (environment variable) databaseName: Internal reference name (snake_case database + snapshot date) databaseLabel: Pretty name output formatting cdmDatabaseSchema: Schema containing OMOP CDM tables (format: schema database.schema) vocabDatabaseSchema: Schema containing vocabulary tables (usually cdmDatabaseSchema) workDatabaseSchema: Schema user write access (cohort tables intermediary work) tempEmulationSchema: Optional schema temp tables (snowflake, oracle) cohortTable: Name cohort table create (default: {repoName}_{databaseName}) Example config.yml Snowflake PostgreSQL: Setting environment variables: .Renviron file (project home directory): Load running pipeline: readRenviron("~/.Renviron")
code: user: !expr Sys.getenv('dbUser')        # Evaluates R code: retrieves DB_USER from environment password: !expr Sys.getenv('dbPassword') # Evaluates R code: retrieves DB_PASSWORD from environment # Config File for my_study  default:   projectName: my_study   version: 1.0.0  # Snowflake: Uses connectionString format snowflake_prod:   dbms: snowflake   connectionString: !expr Sys.getenv('dbConnectionString')   user: !expr Sys.getenv('dbUser')   password: !expr Sys.getenv('dbPassword')   databaseName: snowflake_prod_20260101   databaseLabel: Snowflake Production   cdmDatabaseSchema: omop_schema   vocabDatabaseSchema: omop_schema   workDatabaseSchema: work_schema   tempEmulationSchema: temp_schema   cohortTable: my_study_cohorts  # PostgreSQL: Uses server/port format postgres_local:   dbms: postgresql   server: localhost   port: 5432   user: !expr Sys.getenv('pgUser')   password: !expr Sys.getenv('pgPassword')   databaseName: postgres_local_20260101   databaseLabel: PostgreSQL Local   cdmDatabaseSchema: public   vocabDatabaseSchema: public   workDatabaseSchema: results   cohortTable: my_study_pg_cohorts dbUser=your_db_username dbPassword=your_db_password dbConnectionString=jdbc:snowflake://account.snowflakecomputing.com:443 pgUser=postgres_user pgPassword=postgres_password

Tasks start cohorts generated. See Running Pipeline details pipeline execution cohort generation. Tasks named sequentially (01_, 02_, etc.) executed order: task independent can tested individually development using testStudyTask(). Task File Format Validation: Picard enforces standardized task file structure ensure consistency enable add-modules import external task files. Use makeTaskFile() create new tasks—automatically generates files correct format. Required task file sections (validated validateStudyTask()): . Meta: Metadata task (title, author, description, purpose) B. Dependencies: Input files objects required task C. Connection Settings: Configuration block name pipeline version (uses template variables !||configBlock||! !||pipelineVersion||!) Create executionSettings <- createExecutionSettingsFromConfig(configBlock = configBlock) Create outputFolder <- setOutputFolder(executionSettings = executionSettings, pipelineVersion = pipelineVersion, taskName = "task_name") E. Script: Actual analysis code performs task Validation Rules: task files must pass validation execution: File must exist readable five sections (, B, C, D, E) must present Template variables !||configBlock||! !||pipelineVersion||! must defined section C Section D must create executionSettings object via createExecutionSettingsFromConfig() Section D must create outputFolder object via setOutputFolder() Section E must contain actual code (just comments template placeholders) Validation Matters: Standardized task files enable: Consistency: tasks follow structure across study Portability: Add-modules packages can import external tasks conform format Reproducibility: Clear documentation dependencies configuration Automation: pipeline can reliably execute tasks knowing meet structural requirements Example: task follows required format, import another Picard study add-package rather rewriting .

title: The Ulysses Standard Repository Structure
text: Cohort definitions defining study populations. cohort set persons satisfy one inclusion criteria duration time. OHDSI research, cohorts foundation observational studies. Cohort Definition Types: Uses CIRCE-Java library standardized representation Stored JSON files json/ folder Ensures consistent serialization SQL: definition always generates identical populations Typically imported ATLAS Manual SQL queries sql/ folder Used CIRCE express logic need Subsets existing cohorts (apply additional inclusion criteria) Unions multiple cohorts (combine populations) Stored sql/ folder derivative definitions Folder Structure: cohortsLoad.csv: CSV file metadata cohort. loadCohortManifest() called, file used enrich CohortDef objects matching file_name actual cohort files. Used track cohorts came organize tags. Columns: - atlasId: ATLAS cohort ID (integer, e.g., 1, 42) - label: Display name (character, e.g., "Type 2 Diabetes patients") - category: Broad grouping (character, e.g., "Disease Populations") - subCategory: Sub-grouping (character, optional, e.g., "Endocrine") - file_name: Relative path JSON file (character, e.g., "json/t2dm_patients.json") Example: loaded, metadata fields converted tags cohort later querying (e.g., $queryCohortsByTag(), $queryCohortsByLabel()). cohortManifest.sqlite: SQLite database created/managed CohortManifest class. Contains cohort_manifest table tracking cohort metadata: Workflow: Create cohortsLoad.csv metadata cohorts (use createBlankCohortsLoadFile()) Import ATLAS cohort JSON definitions json/ folder (use importAtlasCohorts()) Add custom SQL dependency-based cohorts sql/ (e.g., subsets, unions) Call loadCohortManifest() scan directories enrich cohortsLoad.csv metadata First load creates cohortManifest.sqlite database; subsequent loads verify file hashes Use CohortManifest methods query cohorts: $queryCohortById(1), $queryCohortsByTag("category: Disease Populations") See also: Loading Inputs detailed guidance creating, importing, managing cohort manifests.
code: inputs/cohorts/ ├── json/ │   ├── 001_primaryPopulation.json     # CIRCE-based from ATLAS │   ├── 002_comparativeArm.json        # CIRCE-based from ATLAS │   └── 003_outcomeDefinition.json     # CIRCE-based from ATLAS ├── sql/ │   ├── 004_primarySubset.sql          # Subset of cohort 001 │   └── 005_combinedPopulation.sql     # Union of cohorts 001 & 002 ├── cohortsLoad.csv                    # Metadata index for cohort enrichment └── cohortManifest.sqlite              # Provenance & metadata tracking database atlasId,label,category,subCategory,file_name 1,Type 2 Diabetes,Disease Populations,Endocrine,json/t2dm_patients.json 2,Diabetes Complications,Disease Populations,Endocrine,json/diabetes_complications.json

title: The Ulysses Standard Repository Structure
text: Stores CIRCE-based concept set definitions identifying cohorts extracting covariates: conceptSetsLoad.csv: Similar cohortsLoad.csv, CSV provides metadata enriching ConceptSetDef objects. Columns: - atlasId: ATLAS concept set ID (integer, e.g., 456, 789) - label: Display name (character, e.g., "Antidiabetic medications") - category: Broad grouping (character, e.g., "Medications" "Diagnoses") - subCategory: Optional sub-grouping (character, e.g., "Endocrine Drugs") - domain: OMOP clinical domain (required, character): - drug_exposure - Medication/drug concept sets - condition_occurrence - Diagnosis concept sets - measurement - Lab/test result concept sets - procedure - Medical procedure concept sets - observation - Observation concept sets - device_exposure - Device/equipment concept sets - visit_occurrence - Visit type concept sets - init - yet classified (placeholder) - sourceCode: Rarely used; TRUE concept set represents source codes instead standard concepts (character: TRUE FALSE) - file_name: Relative path JSON file (character, e.g., "json/hypertension.json") Example: loaded, metadata fields converted tags concept set querying (e.g., $getConceptSetsByTag(), $getConceptSetsByLabel()). conceptSetManifest.sqlite: SQLite database created/managed ConceptSetManifest class. Contains concept_set_manifest table: Workflow: Create update conceptSetsLoad.csv metadata (use createBlankConceptSetsLoadFile()) Import ATLAS concept set JSON definitions json/ folder (use importAtlasConceptSets()) Call loadConceptSetManifest() scan directories enrich conceptSetsLoad.csv metadata First load creates conceptSetManifest.sqlite database; subsequent loads verify file hashes Use ConceptSetManifest methods query: $getConceptSetById(456), $getConceptSetsByTag("domain: drug_exposure") Extract source codes dependencies using $extractSourceCodes() (requires ExecutionSettings) See also: Loading Inputs detailed guidance creating, importing, managing concept set manifests.
code: inputs/conceptSets/ ├── json/ │   ├── exposure_antidiabetic.json        # Drug exposure concept set │   ├── outcome_mi.json                   # Condition outcome concept set │   └── covariate_hypertension.json       # Covariate measurement concept set ├── conceptSetsLoad.csv                   # Metadata index for concept set enrichment └── conceptSetManifest.sqlite             # Provenance & metadata tracking database atlasId,label,category,subCategory,domain,sourceCode,file_name 456,Antidiabetic Medications,Medications,Endocrine,drug_exposure,FALSE,json/exposure_antidiabetic.json 789,Acute MI,Diagnoses,Cardiovascular,condition_occurrence,FALSE,json/outcome_mi.json 1001,Hypertension,Diagnoses,Cardiovascular,condition_occurrence,FALSE,json/covariate_hypertension.json

title: The Ulysses Standard Repository Structure
text: Raw results pipeline execution, organized hierarchically: results organized: Database folders: One folder per database configured config.yml. Database folder name snake_case version databaseName config (e.g., optum_dod → optum_dod/) Version folders: Within database, results organized pipelineVersion (e.g., 1.0.0/, 1.0.1/). special dev/ folder holds temporary test results Task folders: Within version, one folder per task created setOutputFolder() (e.g., 00_buildCohorts/, 01_descriptiveStats/) Task file execution: task script analysis/tasks/ creates output folder via: creates: exec/results/{databaseName}/{pipelineVersion}/{taskName}/ task results (CSVs, logs, etc.) written. Development vs. Production: - Development mode (test_main.R): Uses pipelineVersion = "dev" results go exec/results/{db}/dev/. Results temporary don’t interfere versioned runs. - Production mode (main.R): Uses semantic versioning (e.g., pipelineVersion = "1.0.0") results go exec/results/{db}/1.0.0/. Results retained post-processing archival.

text: R6 class manages collection CohortDef objects maintains metadata SQLite database. CohortManifest class manages multiple cohort definitions stores metadata SQLite database located inputs/cohorts/cohortManifest.sqlite. CohortDef assigned sequential ID based position manifest. Requires executionSettings set includes: database connection (via getConnection() workDatabaseSchema target schema cohortTable desired table name tempEmulationSchema needed database platform Requires executionSettings set includes: database connection (via getConnection() workDatabaseSchema target schema cohortTable desired table name Requires executionSettings set valid database connection, workDatabaseSchema, cohortTable. Execution flow: Build dependency graph CohortDef objects Validate circular dependencies (error found) Topologically sort cohorts dependencies (parents children) cohort topological order: circe cohorts: check SQL hash (existing logic) dependent cohorts: compute dependency hash parent hashes + rule Render execute SQL (circe uses SqlRender parameters, dependent uses metadata JSON) Record checksums dependency hashes database Report results cohort_type, depends_on, dependency_status columns Requires executionSettings set includes: database connection (via getConnection() cdmDatabaseSchema (OMOP CDM data resides) workDatabaseSchema (cohort results written) cohortTable (destination table name) tempEmulationSchema needed database platform

title: ConceptSetManifest R6 Class — ConceptSetManifest
text: R6 class manages collection ConceptSetDef objects maintains metadata SQLite database. ConceptSetManifest class manages multiple concept set definitions stores metadata SQLite database located inputs/conceptSets/conceptSetManifest.sqlite. ConceptSetDef assigned sequential ID based position manifest. Vocabulary Suggestion Domain: function automatically suggests appropriate vocabularies based concept set domains: condition_occurrence: ICD10CM, ICD9CM procedure: HCPCS, CPT4 measurement: LOINC drug_exposure: NDC observation: vocabularies (ICD9CM, ICD10CM, HCPCS, CPT4, LOINC, NDC) device_exposure: NDC visit_occurrence: ICD10CM, ICD9CM, HCPCS, CPT4 Note: suggestions based OMOP CDM conventions. can override valid vocabulary combination. Processing Workflow: Verifies ExecutionSettings configured database connection Detects domains concept sets manifest Displays suggested vocabularies based detected domains Prompts user accept override suggested vocabularies Creates new xlsx workbook concept set manifest: Reads CIRCE JSON definition Builds concept query selecting standard concepts (using CirceR) Performs SQL join: concepts -> concept_relationship (Maps ) -> source concepts Finds matching source codes specified vocabularies Adds results new sheet xlsx workbook formatted header Provides status messages concept set Exports combined results {outputFolder}/SourceCodeWorkbook.xlsx sheet contains columns: vocabulary_id, concept_code, concept_name Sheet headers styled blue background white bold text Column widths auto-fitted readability SQL Query Pattern: concept set, following logic executed: CTE selects standard concepts concept set Joins concept_relationship table relationship_id = 'Maps ' Maps relationship finds source codes map standard concepts Filters valid, non-invalid source codes specified vocabularies Results ordered vocabulary_id concept_code Requirements: ExecutionSettings must initialized valid database connection Vocabulary schema must accessible ExecutionSettings openxlsx2 package must installed User must READ permissions vocabulary tables Error Handling: Displays warnings concept set processing fails continues others Provides clear error messages database connection unavailable Validates source vocabularies known vocabulary IDs function identifies standard concepts included concept set finding reverse mapping relationship. concept set: Reads CIRCE JSON definition Builds concept query using CirceR Joins concept_relationship via reverse "Maps " relationship (finds maps concept set concepts) Filters standard concepts (standard_concept = 'S') Adds results new sheet xlsx workbook Exports results {outputFolder}/IncludedCodes.xlsx sheet contains: concept_id, concept_name, vocabulary_id Requirements: ExecutionSettings must initialized valid connection Vocabulary schema must accessible ExecutionSettings openxlsx2 package must installed

title: picard 0.0.3.1
text: minor bug fixes $addCirceCohort add circe cohort json folder manifest

title: picard 0.0.3
text: CohortManifest fully redesigned R6 class backed SQLite. cohort lifecycle management — adding, building, querying, syncing, deleting, generating — now encapsulated methods class. previous functional approach (manifest_cohorts.R, manifest_conceptSets.R) removed.

title: picard 0.0.3
text: $addAtlasCohort(atlasId, label, category, tags, atlasConnection) — fetch single cohort JSON ATLAS register $addCaprCohort(caprCohort, label, category, tags) — export Capr Cohort object JSON register $addSqlCohort(filePath, label, category, tags) — register existing .sql file; validates portability $importAtlasCohorts(atlasConnection, cohortsLoadPath) — batch import via cohortsLoad.csv; extra columns become tags $setAtlasConnection(atlasConnection) / $getAtlasConnection() — store connection manifest need passed every call getAtlasConnection() now exported standalone function (replaces setAtlasConnection() deprecated)

title: picard 0.0.3
text: dependent cohort methods now CohortManifest class. Dependency metadata stored SQLite (depends_on, dependency_rule columns); sidecar JSON files. parent’s SQL file changes, downstream derived cohorts automatically marked stale. $buildUnionCohort(label, cohortIds, category, gapDays) — era-collapse union ≥2 cohorts $buildComplementCohort(label, populationCohortId, excludeCohortIds, category, complementType) — exclude subjects; updated signature; supports "exclude_any" (default) "exclude_all" $buildCompositeCohort(label, cohortIds, category, minCohorts) — intersection requiring membership ≥ minCohorts $buildSubsetCohortTemporal(label, baseCohortId, filterCohortId, category, startWindow, endWindow, endDateType, subsetLimit) — temporal subset using SubsetWindowOperator objects $buildDemographicCohort(label, baseCohortId, category, minAge, maxAge, genderConceptIds, raceConceptIds, ethnicityConceptIds) — new: filter base cohort person-level demographics $buildStratifiedCohorts(baseCohortId, strata, labelPrefix, category) — new: split cohort N named strata; automatically appends Unclassified stratum covering non-matching subjects Removed: addDependentCohort() (replaced entirely build*() methods )

title: picard 0.0.3
text: $tabulateManifest(filter) — tabulate manifest tibble; filter now accepts "active" (default), "deleted", "stale", "" $syncManifest() — reconcile files disk (json/, sql/) SQLite; flags missing files deleted, detects hash changes, reports unregistered files $reviewDependentCohorts() — new: tibble derived cohorts parsed parent labels rule summaries $reviewStaleCohorts() — new: list cohorts marked stale (parent SQL changed since last build); re-executed automatically executeCohortGeneration() $reloadFromDb() — new: refresh -memory manifest SQLite (useful external DB changes resetCohortManifest()) $statusReport() — tabular status overview active cohorts dependencies $validateManifest() — check file presence active cohort records $cleanupMissing(keep_trace) — soft-delete (keep_trace = TRUE) hard-remove (keep_trace = FALSE) cohorts whose files missing

title: picard 0.0.3
text: $deleteCohort(id, reason) — soft delete: marks status = 'deleted', preserves SQLite record file disk; recoverable $removeCohort(id, deleteFile, dropFromCohortTable, confirm) — new: hard, irreversible removal; deletes SQLite record; optionally deletes file disk (deleteFile = TRUE) /drops rows DBMS cohort checksum tables (dropFromCohortTable = TRUE, requires executionSettings); requires interactive confirmation confirm = TRUE Removed: permanentlyDeleteCohort() hardDeleteCohort() — consolidated removeCohort()

title: picard 0.0.3
text: $createCohortTables() — create cohort-related DBMS tables (main, inclusion, stats, checksum); skips tables already exist $dropCohortTables(tableTypes) — drop cohort tables; optionally limit specific table types $cleanCohortTable() — every status = 'deleted' cohort, delete DBMS rows mark manifest record 'purged' $executeCohortGeneration() — generate cohorts topological dependency order; skips cohorts whose checksum unchanged; marks stale derived cohorts re-execution

title: picard 0.0.3
text: resetCohortManifest(scope) now supports three scopes: "derived" — drop derived cohort rows SQLite delete derived/ SQL files; leaves base cohorts json//sql/ intact "manifest" — delete entire SQLite database; leaves files disk "full" — delete SQLite, delete derived/, delete json/ sql/ directories, drop DBMS cohort tables

title: picard 0.0.3
